steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage describes a cause-and-effect sequence: the plant absorbs and stores cadmium from the soil, and as a result, soil cadmium concentrations decrease. The transition must show that the second clause follows as a consequence of the first.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Specifically': signals that the second clause is a specific instance of the first — but a drop in soil cadmium is a consequence, not a specific example of plant absorption. ✗
  • Choice B — 'In turn': signals that one event leads to or produces another — correctly showing the soil concentration drop as the result of the plant's absorption. ✓
  • Choice C — 'In addition': treats both clauses as separate, unrelated facts rather than cause and effect. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Nevertheless': signals contradiction or contrast — the opposite of what the passage expresses. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
